summary: CMS/site-content feature + full regression tested. Backend: 63/63 tests pass across three files (test_cms.py 17, test_ojs_api.py 34, test_seaipc_features.py 12). Added comprehensive CMS backend suite covering GET /api/content (public, _id excluded, default seeded), PUT /api/content (admin-only with author & editor both 403, partial merge preserves other sections), POST /api/content/logo/upload (admin gate, png/svg accepted, gif rejected, public GET /api/public/logo/{id} inline disposition, 404 for unknown), POST /api/content/flyer/upload (png & pdf OK, svg rejected, admin gate). Frontend UI: admin login ? Sidebar 'Site Content' (nav-cms) ? /dashboard/cms loads with all 7 tabs (theme/branding/flyer/dates/about/cfp/templates); 8/8 palette cards visible; clicking Rose + Save live-updates CSS var --brand from #002FA7 to #BE123C; theme persists across navigation to public Home (home hero brand color = #BE123C); theme restored to blue-classic. Branding tab shows hero_title/stat inputs + logo upload input. Flyer tab has enable switch + title/caption + upload input. Dates tab shows 6 seeded items + Add button. CFP tab shows 39 sub-theme inputs + publications. Templates tab shows 2 seeded template items. Public /about, /call-for-papers, /dates, /templates all render ContentProvider data. Flyer section correctly ABSENT on Home when flyer.enabled=false. No CORS/auth regressions � Bearer-only auth works; existing 46 tests still green.
action_items: (Optional, non-blocking) CMS.jsx is 520 lines with 7 tab sub-components inline - consider splitting each Tab component into its own file under src/pages/cms/ for maintainability.

(Optional) PUT /api/content currently does a full top-level $set replace for each included key (e.g., sending branding={hero_title:'x'} wipes other branding keys). Current frontend CMS.jsx handles this correctly by always sending the full nested object, but document this contract or do deep-merge server-side to harden against partial branding PUTs from future API clients.
(Optional) Logo/flyer upload has no image-dimension or aspect-ratio validation - an admin uploading a 50px � 50px logo could look bad in PublicHeader. Consider a minimum-size check or auto-resize.

(Optional) _upload_content_image() stores by extension, not by sniffing magic bytes � a malicious admin could upload something mislabelled as .png. Low risk since only admins can upload, but worth a note.
(Optional, carry-over) server.py now 883 lines � split into routers (auth, papers, content, users) if growth continues.
critical_code_review_comments: GET /api/content re-merges DEFAULT_CONTENT with the DB doc on every request using a shallow {**DEFAULT_CONTENT, **c} merge. If the DB doc has cfp={sub_themes:[...partial]}, the partial shallow merge will NOT fall back to default sub_themes for missing nested fields. Works today because the seed writes the full doc, but any future schema addition (new default field) won't be backfilled for existing deployments. Consider deep-merge or migration on startup.

PUT /api/content trusts body.branding/about/cfp as 'dict' without Pydantic validation of nested shape. An admin sending branding={'logo_url': 123} (int instead of str) would persist and later break the frontend render. Consider nested Pydantic models for each section.

_upload_content_image stores uploaded files in db.files mixed with paper files (differentiated only by 'kind'). Consider a separate collection (db.content_files) to avoid accidental cross-joins in paper queries.

GET /api/public/logo/{id} has no cache headers (Cache-Control, ETag) � every PublicHeader render fetches full bytes. Logos change rarely � adding Cache-Control: public, max-age=3600 would reduce load.
Frontend ContentProvider likely refetches on every route mount (need to verify) � if so, add a simple in-memory cache or SWR to avoid redundant /api/content calls on every navigation.
Axios withCredentials removal fixed CORS but also means any future cookie-based feature (e.g., CSRF token) will silently fail � document the Bearer-only contract in README to prevent regression.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_cms.py (new - 17 tests for CMS/content endpoints, logo/flyer upload, admin authz, partial merge)

success_rate.backend: 100% (63/63) � 17/17 new CMS tests + 34/34 OJS regression + 12/12 SEAIPC regression
success_rate.frontend: 100% smoke-tested: login + all 7 CMS tabs + 8 palettes + live theme switch + 4 public pages

test_credentials: admin@ojs.com/admin123, editor@ojs.com/editor123, reviewer@ojs.com/reviewer123, author@ojs.com/author123 - all verified via /auth/me
seed_data_creation: Tests do CREATE->GET->DELETE pattern and restore state: theme changed to 'rose' then restored to original; branding hero_title temporarily set to TEST_TITLE_* then restored; dates list temporarily extended with TEST_date then restored. Uploaded logo/flyer files persist in db.files (kind='logo'/'flyer') � not cleaned up since no delete endpoint exists but they don't affect functional tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Three backend test files: test_ojs_api.py (34 OJS CRUD), test_seaipc_features.py (12 password-reset/DOI/preview), test_cms.py (17 CMS/content). Run all: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://paper-review-flow.preview.emergentagent.com pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v. Flaky: test_ojs_api.py::TestAuth::test_me occasionally fails with connect timeout to Emergent ingress (intermittent DNS/CF-ray issue) � retry usually passes. Known infra behavior: Emergent ingress sets Access-Control-Allow-Origin:* AND Access-Control-Allow-Credentials:true (normally invalid), so frontend MUST NOT use withCredentials in axios (E1 already fixed). Logo/flyer upload depends on Emergent object storage (X-Storage-Key header) which has intermittent 500s � tests skip gracefully on 'Storage unavailable'. CMS flyer requires flyer.enabled=true AND flyer.image_url for Home rendering; leaving flyer disabled is the default and Home correctly hides the section.
rca of the issue: N/A - no bugs found. One transient network timeout on test_me during full-suite run (re-passed on retry); ingress/DNS hiccup, not a code issue.
